reviews.review_description: If its possible to use this in a large open area without the safety boxes that would be amazing. As it is, its very immersive and I can see the potential for great things. Where there were rocks on the floor my mind was warning me about tripping on them. Can't wait for the meta ankle bracelets lol

reviews.review_title: Develop the camping app first, using thi
reviews.review_description:
s tech
it's a really nice tech demo

I can't stand games or experiences that don't give you the option to smoothly move about the environment. Being restricted to teleporting is hopelessly lame, and an excellent way to prevent you from becoming immersed.

I had the feeling that I was really in the forest, the visuals are perfect. a lot of great games could be made on this principle, where it is not necessary to perform the rear action at the same time as walking. am great idea for improvement! instead of a teleport, I would give something better. You can't walk in VR if you don't have a lot of space around you. what cannot be realized should be replaced as best as possible. I would imagine that I point to one of the possible places for teleportation, then it automatically turns me as needed and starts a pre-recorded 3D 180° video where I walk to that place. after the end of the video, it takes me in a new position where I was watching at the end of the video. It would be great !

reviews.review_title: What!? How is this so real?!
reviews.review_description:
How is this visually possible on Quest 3 standalone?! I looks almost indistinguishable from reality. I went in my backyard and walked around freely for 30 minutes in disbelief, when I took it off I felt like I just tripped out of another dimension.

Just browsing AppLab and this is the BEST tech demo I've ever experienced. I am showing this to everyone immediately 😮😱❤️‍🔥

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing feedback! 

We’re thrilled to hear that our tech demo left such a strong impression on you. And just wait until you see our next release – it’s going to be even more mind-blowing! Get ready for some serious fun.

Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Often, when I am in a VR environment, I know it is VR because it doesn’t feel immersive.

This tech demo shows potential for the future.

reviews.review_title: Amazing, wish UI could be hidden
reviews.review_description: This is a fantastic tech demo, incredibly impressive. My only request: allow users to completely disable UI elements. It's possible to disable the large white bounding boxes, but I'd love an option to disable the teleport markers and the large UI panels at the end (that completely block visuals to the diner). Also, I'd suggest an option to disable the hovercar as it detracts from the rest of the impressive visuals.

developer_response.body:
Hey Mike, thank you very much for your review!

It's crucial for us to understand the feedback from users who try our tech demo, as it allows us to continually refine and enhance future versions of Oniri.

We greatly value your suggestions and assure you that we will seriously consider the possibility of allowing users to completely disable the UI elements in upcoming releases. 

Stay tuned for future updates, and thank you again for your support!

reviews.review_description: Hyper realistic... wish all vr apps were on this level of quality... one day!

Best graphics I've seen on Quest. However, those locomotion cubes are extremely immersion-breaking imo. I suggest displaying them only when holding the locomotion button (stick). Also, there is a lot of performance headroom on Quest 3, I was able to increase the resolution by 150% without stuttering - after applying this setting, it was truly a next-gen experience. I keep fingers crossed for you guys!

The application is decent. It looks cool. It's basically like a bunch of pictures stuck together so you can move through it. Not a lot of motion happening. Only issue is I have installed it and now I cannot remove it over my wish list or saved list on the Quest app

reviews.review_title: 1 Star because I can see the potential
reviews.review_description: As the title suggests, I can see this being a very impressive tech demo, but unfortunately, the experience loads and is basically broke. The environment is there but stretched and running at about 1FPS, but yes, it does look impressive from what i can kind of see. I just wish I could experience it. I'm using a Quest 3, but it doesn't seem to run.

developer_response.body:
First of all, thank you for the feedback!

It appears to be a bug affecting a limited number of users and seems to be related to the original room-scale setup. Switching to the "standing" mode (where you don't set room boundaries but a circle is automatically formed around you) appears to resolve the issue.

That being said, we are already working on fixing this bug for the next release.

In the meantime, if you'd like, the procedure mentioned above can bypass the current bug!

Let me know if this current turnaround works for you and thanks for your understanding.

reviews.review_description: A super cool tech demo, but it genuinely only took me about 15 seconds to explore the full thing. Also there’s just a random car which doesn’t blend into the environment at all.
Meta VR tech is incredible
On quest 2 and 3

developer_response.body:
First of all, thank you for the feedback!

It appears to be a bug affecting a limited number of users and seems to be related to the original room-scale setup. Switching to the "standing" mode (where you don't set room boundaries but a circle is automatically formed around you) appears to resolve the issue.

Some users have informed us that updating the Quest's software has also solved the problem for them.

Let me know if this current turnaround works for you and thanks for your understanding.

reviews.review_description: Completely unusable: the image jitters violently, the scene moves with the head, 4 controllers appear in the air. I had trouble getting back to the main menu to End it.

developer_response.body:
First of all, thank you for the feedback!

It appears to be a bug affecting a limited number of users and seems to be related to the original room-scale setup. Switching to the "standing" mode (where you don't set room boundaries but a circle is automatically formed around you) appears to resolve the issue.

Some users have informed us that updating the Quest's software has also solved the problem for them.

Let me know if this current turnaround works for you and thanks for your understanding.

reviews.review_title: Beautiful! Now give me a PC screen!
reviews.review_description: Absolutely stunning! I really, really want to make this my new VR workspace. Just let me turn off the UI elements & mirror a PC screen somewhere so I can work in there, and allow me to give you my money!
Short but super realistic

This demo is INSANE. I've tried other nature apps like Real VR Fishing and Brink Traveler but nothing comes close to the realism this app offers. It literally feels REAL. I simply can't believe this is running on the quest. I would LOVE to see more of these demos in different nature environments and an app with a bunch of nature scenes like this one to visit like a traveling app! However, one thing I would love to see in this app is more beautiful nature environments and the freedom to explore more instead of designated locations. Also, it does stutter a bit when moving on my quest 2.

UPDATE: Thanks for the response! Even with mild stuttering, I'd still rather have high quality graphics than lower the quality. It's so worth it. Also, I would specifically LOVE to see a nature app (such as BRINK Traveler) with these types of nature environments. I'll be a dream come true!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback!

Your comment is incredibly rewarding and validate the hard work our R&D team has put into developing Oniri over the past year.

We're excited to let you know that we're already working on new demos to showcase how the Oniri technology can be applied not only to natural environments but also to cultural heritage sites, offering ultra-high-detail 3D scans accessible to anyone on their standalone headset and via the web. Additionally, we're exploring applications in various fields like video games, shopping, art, and much more!

Regarding the performance on Quest 2, we've currently standardized the graphic level across both Quest 2 and Quest 3. Based on user feedback like yours, we're considering introducing graphic differentiation in our next release to optimize the experience!

reviews.review_description: WOW! This tech demo was incredible! Imagine the possibilities, like playing paint ball in vr in a photo realistic 3D scan of a real life forest. I really hope game developers take this and implement it into their games and apps if possible. There is so much potential for this tech and I really hope people make the best of it. It would also be GAME CHANGING for Quest Home Environments, please please please 🙏🏻 make this possible it would be so incredibly amazing
Looks amazing. Crashed my headset after few minutes. Not ready.
